commands.review_summary:
The PR allows hardlinked source trees in package-manager update/install swap paths while keeping the generic replacement hardlink rejection and adding focused hardlink regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: create a package root with a hardlinked file and run the current staged package swap path that still passes sourceHardlinks: "reject".
commands.review_followup: Contributor proof and maintainer security-boundary review are required before this PR can merge; there is no narrow automation repair to queue from this review.

target.title: fix(update): allow package-manager hardlinks in swaps
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85559.

- allow hardlinked source trees only for package-manager-owned update/install swaps
- keep the generic `movePathWithCopyFallback` hardlink rejection behavior unchanged
- add real hardlink regression coverage for global package updates and isolated package installs

## Real behavior proof

After-fix focused package swap proof from this branch:

```text
pnpm test src/infra/replace-file.test.ts src/infra/package-update-steps.test.ts src/infra/install-package-dir.test.ts

Test Files  3 passed (3)
     Tests  28 passed (28)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 31.53s
```

The new tests create actual filesystem hardlinks with `fs.link()` and exercise the package-manager swap paths that previously rejected npm/pnpm-style package roots:

- global npm update swap with hardlinked existing and staged package files
- isolated package update with a hardlinked existing install and hardlinked staged dependency files
- generic replacement guard still rejects hardlinked sources when `sourceHardlinks: "reject"` is requested

## Verification

```text
git diff --check
```

Result: passed.

```text
pnpm test src/infra/replace-file.test.ts src/infra/package-update-steps.test.ts src/infra/install-package-dir.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
     Tests  28 passed (28)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 31.53s
```

## Security notes

This does not change the default hardlink guard in `movePathWithCopyFallback`. The allowance is scoped to the package-manager update/install paths where npm and pnpm can produce hardlinked package files. Other replacement callers must still opt in explicitly before hardlinked sources are allowed.

## Not tested

I did not run a full npm 11 or pnpm global install/update E2E locally. The focused proof uses real filesystem hardlinks in the same update/install swap code paths and keeps the generic reject guard covered.
